seoTitle: Sunny Corner vs Sunshine — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunny Corner and Sunshine. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunny Corner vs Sunshine: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunny Corner and Sunshine.
heading: Sunny Corner vs Sunshine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunny Corner, New South Wales and Sunshine, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunny Corner has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Sunshine (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunny Corner or Sunshine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunny Corner has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Sunshine scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunny Corner scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Sunshine scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunny Corner or Sunshine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunny Corner has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
